title: X-tra Silky Indian Chana Chana Masala
description: Traditional Indian dish made with silkened chickpeas, coriander, and shio-cure spices, served in a low-carb-friendly version using a tagine pot. This adventurous dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner or special occasion.
instructions: Heat ghee or oil in a tagine pot over medium heat.
Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add ginger and garlic and cook for another minute.
Add coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, red chili powder, salt, and shio-cure salt. Cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add chickpeas and cook for 2-3 minutes, until coated in spice mixture.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Stir in chopped cilantro and lemon juice.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve hot over rice or with naan bread.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
Serve with raita or salad, if desired.
Garnish with crushed cumin or coriander seeds, if desired.
